title: SQL Injection Login Bypass
tags: sqli sql injection login bypass or 1=1 union select --
content: SQL injection often appears in login endpoints where user input is concatenated into SQL. Typical payload patterns include quote breaking, OR true expression, UNION queries, and SQL comments.
evidence: Payload contains patterns like ' or 1=1 --, union select, sleep(), benchmark().
mitigation: Use parameterized queries, strict input validation, WAF signatures, and least-privilege DB accounts.

title: XSS Script Injection
tags: xss cross site scripting script alert onerror svg javascript
content: XSS attacks inject executable script into reflected or stored content, leading to session hijack and browser-side payload execution.
evidence: Request/body contains <script>, javascript:, onerror=, or suspicious HTML event handlers.
mitigation: Apply output encoding, CSP, input sanitization, and framework-level template escaping.

title: Brute Force Credential Attack
tags: bruteforce brute force password spray login retry failed auth
content: Brute force and password spray attacks generate many authentication attempts with changing credentials in short time windows.
evidence: High-frequency login requests from same source IP with repeated failed status codes.
mitigation: Enable rate limit, lockout policy, MFA, and IP reputation controls.

title: DDoS High-Frequency Flood
tags: ddos flood high frequency traffic burst syn flood
content: DDoS behavior usually appears as sudden traffic spikes and repeated requests across one or more endpoints causing service degradation.
evidence: Abnormal request rate, repeated identical paths, elevated 5xx and timeout responses.
mitigation: Apply rate limiting, traffic scrubbing/CDN, autoscaling, and upstream ACL controls.

title: Command Injection
tags: command injection cmd whoami ping && ; | shell
content: Command injection uses shell metacharacters to execute system commands when backend invokes shell with untrusted input.
evidence: Parameters contain command separators like ;, &&, ||, |, backticks, or suspicious shell commands.
mitigation: Avoid shell invocation, use allow-list validation, and run processes with restricted privileges.

title: Path Traversal
content: Path traversal attacks attempt to escape expected directories and read sensitive files.
evidence: URI or parameters include ../, ..\\, encoded traversal sequences, or sensitive file names.
mitigation: Normalize paths, deny parent traversal, and map only approved resource IDs.

title: SSRF Metadata Access
content: SSRF abuses server-side URL fetch functionality to access internal services and cloud metadata endpoints.
evidence: User-controlled URL points to internal IP ranges, localhost, or cloud metadata services.
mitigation: Restrict outbound destinations, enforce URL allow-list, and block metadata endpoints.

title: Malicious File Upload
tags: file upload webshell php jsp aspx executable multipart
content: Attackers may upload webshell or executable payload disguised as normal files.
evidence: Multipart upload contains executable signatures or suspicious extensions with mismatched MIME types.
mitigation: Validate file type/content, store outside webroot, and scan with antivirus engines.

title: Port Scan Reconnaissance
tags: port scan syn probe nmap reconnaissance 22 80 443 3306
content: Port scanning probes multiple ports/services to identify attack surface before exploitation.
evidence: Burst of connection attempts across many destination ports from same source.
mitigation: Use IDS/IPS scan detection, tarpits, and strict ingress filtering policies.

title: API Auth Abuse
tags: api auth token refresh abuse replay credential stuffing
content: Authentication abuse includes token replay, credential stuffing, and repeated refresh or login abuse patterns.
evidence: Frequent auth endpoint hits with unusual headers, repeated failures, or replay signatures.
mitigation: Use device binding, token rotation, anomaly detection, and strict rate control.

title: RCE Exploit Attempt
tags: rce remote code execution template injection jndi deserialization
content: RCE attempts seek direct code execution through vulnerable template engines, deserialization paths, or framework CVEs.
evidence: Payload includes template expressions, class loading chains, or exploit gadget indicators.
mitigation: Patch dependencies, disable dangerous interpreters, and isolate runtime permissions.

title: HTTP 5xx/504 as Risk Signal
content: A high rate of 5xx/504 responses can indicate active attack pressure or service instability. It is not proof alone but should increase risk confidence when combined with malicious payload evidence.
evidence: Request includes suspicious payload and response shows timeout or server error repeatedly.
mitigation: Correlate payload signature + traffic frequency + status anomalies before final severity decision.
